description: Multi-level approval workflow with sequential/parallel approvers, delegation, auto-approve rules, timeout escalation, and audit history.

actors.name: Requester
actors.description: Submits a request that requires one or more levels of approval

actors.name: Approver
actors.description: Reviews and approves or rejects requests at their assigned level

actors.name: Delegate
actors.description: Acts on behalf of an approver who has delegated authority

actors.name: Escalation Target
actors.description: Receives escalated requests when approval times out

actors.name: Approval Engine
actors.description: Manages approval flow, timeout detection, escalation, and auto-approve logic

validation.message: Title must be 500 characters or fewer
At least one approval level is required

values.name: draft
values.description: Request created but not yet submitted for approval

values.name: pending
values.description: Request submitted and waiting for first-level review

values.name: in_review
values.description: Request is being reviewed at the current approval level

values.name: approved
values.description: All approval levels have approved the request

values.name: rejected
values.description: Request was rejected by an approver

values.name: escalated
values.description: Approval timed out and was escalated to a higher authority

values.name: withdrawn
values.description: Requester withdrew the request before completion

transitions.description: Requester submits the request for approval
System routes request to first-level approvers

transitions.condition: current level approved but more levels remain

transitions.description: Last approval level passes, request fully approved
Escalation target takes over the review

level_response_time.escalation: escalate to next approver in hierarchy or designated escalation_target

approval_strategy.description: Each level defines a strategy: "all" requires every approver to approve, "any" requires at least one, "first" accepts the first response (approve or reject). The strategy is evaluated per level independently.

timeout_escalation.description: If no approver at a level responds within timeout_hours, the request is escalated. Escalation routes to the configured escalation_target or the next level's approvers.

delegation.description: An approver can delegate their approval authority to another user for a specified period. The delegate's decisions carry the same weight as the original approver's. Delegations expire automatically after valid_until.

immutable_history.description: Once an approval action is recorded in approval_history, it cannot be modified or deleted. Each entry includes approver, action, comment, and timestamp for audit compliance.

auto_approve.description: Configurable rules can automatically approve a level without human intervention (e.g., requests under a certain amount or from specific roles). Auto-approved entries are logged in history with actor "system".

withdrawal_rules.description: A requester can withdraw their request at any point before the final approval. Withdrawal is recorded in history and notifies all involved approvers.

no_self_approval.description: A requester cannot approve their own request. If the requester appears in an approval level's approver list, they are skipped for that level.

given.description: At least one approval level is configured

then.description: First-level approvers notified of pending request

request_submitted.result: Request submitted and routed to first approval level

given: all approvers at the current level have approved
level_approved_all_strategy.result: Current level approved; request advances to next level or completes

given: at least one approver at the current level has approved
level_approved_any_strategy.result: Current level approved by any single approver; advances

given: first approver at the current level has responded with approve
all approval levels have been approved
current_level equals total number of levels
request_fully_approved.result: Request fully approved across all levels

given: an approver at any level rejects the request

then.description: Requester notified of rejection with reason
Escalation target and requester notified of timeout

given: approver has configured a valid delegation
delegation is within its valid_until period
approval_delegated.result: Approval authority delegated; delegate can act on behalf of approver

given: request matches configured auto-approve rules for current level
auto_approved.result: Level auto-approved by system based on configured rules

errors.message: The approval request does not exist.
This approval level has already been decided.
You are not an authorized approver for this level.
You cannot approve your own request.
This request has already been withdrawn.
The specified approval level configuration is invalid.

events.name: approval.requested
events.description: A new approval request was submitted

events.name: approval.approved
events.description: An approval level or the full request was approved

events.name: approval.escalated
events.description: An approval timed out and was escalated

events.name: approval.delegated
events.description: Approval authority was delegated to another user

related.reason: Tasks requiring sign-off can trigger an approval chain
Expense approval is a specific instance of a generic approval chain
Notify approvers and requesters of status changes
Approver roles and delegation permissions require access control

goals.description: Process approval requests quickly while maintaining compliance and audit integrity

success_metrics.measurement: Time from submission to final decision
Percentage of requests that hit timeout escalation
All decisions have recorded approver, timestamp, and reason

constraints.description: Approval history must be immutable — no retroactive changes to decisions
Approvers can only act on requests within their authorized scope

human_checkpoints: before overriding a rejection at any level
before delegating approval authority across departments

invariants: every approval decision has an identified approver and timestamp
rejected requests cannot proceed without explicit override
delegation chains cannot create circular approval loops
auto-approve rules only apply within configured thresholds

acceptance_tests.given: a request requiring 3 levels of approval
acceptance_tests.expect: request status transitions to approved after final approval

acceptance_tests.given: a request pending for longer than SLA
acceptance_tests.expect: request escalated to next-level approver with notification

acceptance_tests.given: an approver delegates to a substitute
acceptance_tests.expect: audit trail records both original approver and delegate

exposes.contract: accepts {request_id, request_type, metadata}, returns {decision, approver, timestamp}
